programme.title: Supermarket Secrets

Elsewhere, Gregg meets Asda's head of fruit and vegetables to find out why it is a challenge to remove plastic packaging from products, and Nikki Fox sees how they are using a virtual supermarket to see what appeals to us most(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley finds out how crime affects the farming community of Warwickshire and sees some of the technology that helps police track down stolen livestock(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development progress of terraced houses in Carlise and Abbey Wood in London, as well as a seven-year project to renovate a large period house in Torquay(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers investigate the death of a car wash worker, and catch two crooks dealing in counterfeit medicines worth £5million(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A driver whose dashcam recorded his heart attack and rescue by other motorists, and the rescuers who rushed to the aid of people swept out to sea, only to be dragged in themselves(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Tim Weeks provide expert advice to two teams as the competition heads to the Royal Cornwall Showground in Wadebridge. Plus, presenter Anita Manning shows of a Bargain Hunt favourite with a Cornish flavour(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al seeks Zara's advice on making amends with his mother and Becky unearths the tragic secret behind an unusual occurrence at her grandma sheltered housing complex. Emma tries to prevent Karen finding out she has been dreaming about Rob(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple who love the outdoors find the perfect rural family home within reach of the coast, on a budget of £850,000(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich Brothers and Charlie Dimmock compete to create a garden in Chester that combines exotic holiday memories of Borneo, America and South Africa for owners Michelle and Andrew(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton's penultimate leg takes them to Lowestoft and Cromer. Along the way, Izzie is charmed by a snake and an art deco necklace, while James goes crabbing and gets all fired up by a cannonball. But who will come out on top at auction in Banbury?(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bobby tries to come to terms with everything that has happened - only to find himself in a dangerous situation. Jay lets Mel into the funeral parlour to be with Hunter - and when Lisa finds her, the two take a trip down memory lane. Bex tries to open up to a drunken Robbie, and Keegan arranges a romantic meal for Tiffany(n)

programme.title: Watchdog
programme.desc: Matt Allwright, Steph McGovern and Nikki Fox present consumer investigations, including research into which airlines rank best and worst for cost, complaints and customer service. Three families put leading fitness trackers to some rigorous real world testing, there's a look at how high street names are targeting consumers with unsolicited adverts, and in Rogue Traders, a roofer is caught charging thousands of pounds for poor work(n)

programme.title: The Cameron Years
programme.desc: The first in a two-part documentary drawing on an interview with former prime minister David Cameron to explore his leadership, the events that led to the EU referendum and its impact on the political landscape - not to mention his own career. The first film examines how the prime minister went from riding high after the successful 2015 election campaign to defeat in the referendum. Including contributions from Nick Clegg, Iain Duncan Smith, Michael Gove, George Osborne, Ann Widdecombe and Michael Heseltine(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Southampton, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: The second edition of the TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU. And after a dramatic few weeks in the Commons as the deadline approaches, they'll no doubt have much to talk about(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley visits the Metropolitan Police's Mounted Unit, who are celebrating 100 years at their Surrey training centre. Back in the studio, Rav Wilding has the latest appeals for viewers' help(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An update on an unusual project in Capel Le Ferne in Kent that Lucy Alexander first visited in 2009. Plus, a mid-terrace house in Liverpool and a cottage in Plaistow, east London(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The enforcement officers investigate the tragic death of a Red Arrows pilot, and they smash a puppy-smuggling operation. A supermarket is fined £8million after a massive fuel leak at one of its petrol stations has a devastating impact on the local community and environment.(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles tells the story of a paraglider hanging by a thread in a forest after his canopy collapses. A dash cam captures the moment three cars collide on a mountain road, and a bullock on the pitch nearly hits a village cricket team for six(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: As Emma's dreams escalate, Zara has a radical idea about how to stop them, and Becky puts Daniel on the spot. Ayesha deals with the fallout when an angry patient ignores the warning signs that his health is in danger(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Seventeen contenders start this edition of the quiz, but how many of them will survive to face the £10,000 Question? Hosted by Rick Edwards(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton accompanies a couple on a North Yorkshire house hunt. They are calling time on running a guest house in the city and have a £650 000 budget to find a rural family home with business potential. Whilst in the county, Alistair also pays a visit to a heritage train line and helps the team to get their impressive locomotives fired up for the start of the tourist season(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Oxfordshire mum Sarah is after a space where she can relax and unwind after a full-on day minding two young children, but is also aware that the garden also needs to work as a play space for the youngsters. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to Bicester to assist Sarah and partner Adam, who need a garden the whole family can play and relax in(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton tour Izzie's native Derbyshire on the final leg, where she tempts the gourmand with some local delicacies. There's a blast from the past as James is carried away by the news from the 1780s while Izzie follows in the tracks of Winston Churchill. Then, it's onwards to auction in Lincoln(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich brothers and Arit Anderson travel to Shefford in Bedfordshire, where they meet a couple who claim their garden is basic and plain. Duncan would like it to be modern, but also contain elements of a rustic, cottage garden. Both he and Karen like the idea of a curved patio and wish to avoid decking. Will the couple choose the Rich brothers proposed design, complete with bespoke veg beds, pair of curved hedges and paved courtyard, or will Arit's design that offers a new circular lawn, choice of paved seating areas and a vertical waterfall be more to their taste?(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Burnley defender James Tarkowski, Great Britain sprinter Asha Philip, IPC World Championships long jump champion Stef Reid and Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Michael Jamieson join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Lisa supports Mel as she prepares for Hunter's funeral. Over at the Mitchells', however, Phil is adamant that none of his family will attend, leaving Louise conflicted - and things only get worse when Phil realises that Jay went behind his back to help Mel. Meanwhile, Tiffany and Keegan agree to take things slowly, while Patrick is determined to talk to Ted about making a pass at Sheree - only to realise he misses Joyce(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The latest battle for a semi-final place reaches its climax as the four celebrities are tasked to cook a faultless two-course meal that will not only be judged by John Torode and Gregg Wallace but also by former contenders Lisa Faulkner, Kimberly Wyatt and Adrian Edmondson. Only the best two contestants can come one step closer to winning the title of Celebrity MasterChef 2019. For those who don't make the grade, the journey ends here(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: On the day of Mrs Bookham's funeral, a blazing row about the contents of the deceased's handbag sees Lisa Marie storm out telling Geraldine she can 'stick her job'. At the arcade Mike receives a visit from a disgruntled Tony Peroni who wants to call in his debt, while suspicions mounts about Bigsy's involvement in Mr Ferris's burglary. Karen has doubts about her relationship with Mike and during a heart to heart with Lisa Marie confesses that there was a lot more to the kiss between Mike and Hayley Cox. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole, Maggie Ollerenshaw, Catherine Tyldesley and Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Room 101 - Extra Storage
programme.desc: Extended edition of the comedy panel show presented by Frank Skinner, with Richard Ayoade, Alun Cochrane and Gabby Logan competing to have their pet hates consigned to Room 101, with topics including people who laugh out loud when reading a book, fun and half and half scarves(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: An elderly amateur sleuth who was a friend of Danny's is murdered, and he ends up embroiled in the case when his photo is found at the scene. However, the cold case copper becomes convinced that the secret to uncovering her real killer lies within the last case she was working on. Picking up where she left off, he travels to the village of Minchampton, where a woman was said to have been murdered by a local lad 25 years previously. Meanwhile, Sasha is left to cope with the investigating officer on the modern-day Miss Marple's murder - an overbearing contemporary from her Hendon training, by the name of DCI Grace Mackie - and Steve lets Gerry stay with him when he is evicted, but soon comes to regret the act of kindness(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton take a tour of Northamptonshire and Bedfordshire. Items up for sale include a ring made of lava from Mount Vesuvius and depleted uranium glassware(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and Harry and David Rich come up with plans for a Swansea couple who want to redesign their garden to be safe for their children and allow them to grow vegetables(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the life of the hippopotamus as never seen before. The programme gets closer than ever to an animal that cannot swim yet is utterly dependent on water. In Botswana's Okavango delta, hippos face an unparalleled challenge - deep floodwaters dry to dust in a matter of months. In one extraordinary season, cameras go beneath the surface to see them protect their families and face enemies as they deal with the drought. Going far beyond their dangerous reputation, the programme discovers the true nature of the hippo, an animal that is compassionate, sensitive and highly intelligent(n)

programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: A couple from Nottingham and a mother and daughter from Wiltshire sample new lifestyles as they swap homes. After the trial, they reveal what they have learned from the experience(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Return of the programme that follows the work of probate detectives. A man travels 9,000 miles to meet his half-sister for the first time, while a surprise adoption and travelling ancestors threaten to derail the search for a Leicester City fan's heirs(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits archaeological digs in the north of Britain, where finds include evidence for the first Roman siege in the UK. There's also a look at Lindisfarne, which was violently sacked by the Vikings 1,000 years ago(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actress Tamzin Outhwaite sets out to learn about her Italian roots on the maternal side of her family as a result of her fond childhood memories of big meals with relatives, antipasto and energetic conversations. Her journey begins in the heart of London's East End, where her great-grandfather Tony Gonella once ran a cafe. She visits the Isle of Man, Italy and finally Glasgow as she explores her ancestor's life, and also discovers more about her mother's father Remo Santi and his dad Adelmo(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Will Axon and Christina Trevanion assess items at Ragley Hall in Warwickshire, including some intriguing decorative pictures, while Paul Martin looks at the man who saved the hall(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Sam & Shauna's Big Cook Out
programme.desc: Samantha Evans and Shauna Guinn celebrate Cardiff's Gujarati community with a vegetarian cook-out, building a special giant multi-layered grill that several people can use at once to cook all the vegetables. The duo learn how to make an avocado and mango chutney using a recipe that has been handed down through the generations(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests with interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest news on Brexit(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: A 100-year-old Nordic rocking chair that its owner's grandparents were given on their wedding day is restored. A mother of pearl shell and a ceramic bulldog are also redeemed(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The four remaining interior designers take part in a commercial design challenge to transform two restaurants in Bristol, as they look to claim their place in the semi-finals. For the first time, Michelle Ogundehin tells them they will have the chance to meet their clients and see the spaces before taking on the design, but also warns them that she needs to see some individual design flair from each of them, as at the end of the challenge, one designer from the losing team will be leaving the competition(n)

programme.title: The Big Hospital Experiment
programme.desc: The young volunteers begin their third week at the Royal Derby Hospital and Senior Nurse Karen gives them more responsibilities, as she sends four volunteers to two new wards. One of the youngest volunteers on the programme is Fin, who, at 19 years old, wasn't sensitive enough to the needs on the Cancer Ward and was asked to leave. Fin and Michael are moved to the Elderly Care Ward, where they are tasked with providing care for 101-year-old patient John, a wartime Spitfire pilot(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: In the final episode, Max looks forward to her graduation party and despite things not going to plan, has a wonderful unique present from Sam and the girls. Comedy-drama, created by and starring Pamela Adlon(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine the case against Alfred Moore, who was sentenced to death for murdering two policemen in Huddersfield in 1951(n)

programme.title: China: A New World Order
programme.desc: Six years ago, a man largely unknown in the West was given one of the most important jobs in the world. China's new President, Xi Jinping, became the leader of a rising superpower, commander of a formidable military and the head of an economy that is crucial to people's lives around the world. This documentary tells the story of his controversial six-year rule, and how he set about transforming China into a tightly controlled state where loyalty to the Communist Party appears to be valued above everything else(n)

programme.title: The Day the Dinosaurs Died
programme.desc: Documentary examining whether an asteroid strike 66 million years ago triggered a worldwide catastrophe, killing off the dinosaurs and wiping out 75 per cent of all life on Earth. Evolutionary biologist Ben Garrod joins a multimillion pound drilling expedition into the exact spot in the Gulf of Mexico that was the point of impact, while palaeopathologist Alice Roberts meets leading scientists to find if the effects could have wiped out dinosaurs across the world almost immediately(n)

programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton head to Lincolnshire, Cambridgeshire and Bedfordshire. James tries out a local delicacy and Izzie tries her hand at a winter sport(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden in Northampton that will allow two children with limited mobility access to the outdoor space(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Morden Hall Park in South London, assessing items including a rare Cartier driver's watch, a self-portrait by John Lennon and Yoko Ono and a plate designed by Pablo Picasso. Jewellery specialist Joanna Hardy examines a diamond studded replica of a brooch originally designed for Wallis Simpson, while glass expert Andy McConnell inspects three early 18th-century pieces bought for just a few pence(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: Pairs of swappers from Norfolk and Essex put their homes and lifestyles under the microscope as they exchange properties. Presented by Gyles Brandreth(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The search is on for heirs to the million pound estate of Douglas McMurrich, an engineer who was survived by his 76-year-old-pet tortoise. Plus, details of two unclaimed estates(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits archaeological digs in the east of Britain. Finds include new revelations from a 3,000-year-old perfectly preserved village near Cambridgeshire, and the theatre where Shakespeare premiered Romeo and Juliet(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Irish comedian Brendan O'Carroll, best known as his alter ego Mrs Brown and star of the BBC sitcom Mrs Brown's Boys, tries to find out the truth about his grandfather's murder, and attempts to uncover the identities of the men responsible. Shopkeeper Peter O'Carroll was killed in October 1920 at the height of the Irish War of Independence, and the Dublin-born actor begins his journey of discovery with only a newspaper article from the time and the words 'shot during the curfew' from Peter's gravestone(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Elizabeth Talbot and Nick Davies examine the best antiques at Milestones Museum in Basingstoke, and Paul Martin takes a tour round a modern cruise ship to find out about the history of the Southampton cruise liners(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Sam & Shauna's Big Cook Out
programme.desc: Samantha Evans and Shauna Guinn head to Ferryside, a close-knit coastal community just eight miles from Carmarthen in south-west Wales. They want to celebrate the work and achievements of Ferryside Lifeboat, a completely independent organisation run entirely by volunteers, and prepare a spectacular fishy feast, cooked in a giant fire pit on the beach(n)

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas is joined by Craig Mitch, Chelcee Grimes and Reece Parkinson to take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts take on the challenges of restoring three cherished family heirlooms, bringing back to life a Victorian cuckoo clock, a 1970s bowl and a 200-year-old sea chest(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King visit a biker bar in Missouri to find out about the origins of Route 66, before heading west, where they visit a roadside museum described as a love letter to the road. They also stop off for lunch in Kansas, sample Yorkshire pudding wraps in Tulsa and finally reach rural Oklahoma, where they cook with members of the Cherokee nation(n)

programme.title: 1944: Should We Bomb Auschwitz?
programme.desc: In 1944 two Jewish prisoners escaped from Auschwitz. After they told the world of the horror of the Holocaust, it raised one of the greatest moral questions of the 20th century - should the Allies bomb the camp? This documentary tells the whole of the story, combining dramatic reconstructions of the arguments that took place at the highest levels with first-hand testimony from survivors to explore a moral dilemma that still resonates today - how to act in the face of genocide(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and a cast of comedy correspondents including Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on the week's news(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban(n)

programme.desc: The four remaining interior designers take part in a commercial design challenge to transform two restaurants in Bristol, as they look to claim their place in the semi-finals. For the first time, Michelle Ogundehin tells them they will have the chance to meet their clients and see the spaces before taking on the design, but also warns them that she needs to see some individual design flair from each of them, as at the end of the challenge, one designer from the losing team will be leaving the competition(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Canadian comedian Katherine Ryan is the latest celebrity to dig into her family's past in the popular genealogy documentary. Her investigation leads her to a Methodist minister and his family in colonial Nova Scotia, cod traders in Newfoundland, and eventually to her English roots in Dorset(n)

programme.title: Untouchable: The Rise and Fall of Harvey Weinstein
programme.desc: The story of how the film producer acquired and deployed his power, charting how he allegedly abused women with seeming impunity throughout his career - from his early days as a music promoter to running movie company Miramax with his brother Bob. Women who came forward to accuse Weinstein of sexual harassment and assault tell their stories in detail, including Rosanna Arquette, Erika Rosenbaum and Paz De La Huerta, while the reporters who broke the story detail his ruthless attempts to preserve power, even as scandal threatened to engulf him(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich Brothers and Charlie Dimmock compete to create a garden in Chester that combines exotic holiday memories of Borneo, America and South Africa for owners Michelle and Andrew(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With the bank holiday weekend looming, Monty Don has plenty of inspiration for gardeners from planting for late summer colour to tidying up herbs and pruning soft fruit. Nick Bailey concludes his journey into the clematis world by looking at the late summer-flowering varieties and the programme travels to Devon to meet a retired biology teacher with a passion for houseleeks(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Time to Eat
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain demonstrates recipes to take the stress out of impressing guests, including raspberry cheesecake croissants, a speedy satay chicken tray bake and a fuss-free cheat's trifle with creamy chia seeds, yoghurt, fresh fruit and sweet brioche. In Walsall, Nadiya's shortcut curry with spicy jackfruit and speedy naan gives a novice cook the family dinner party that she's always dreamed of(n)

programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: Friends from Cheshire swap homes and lifestyles with a Derbyshire couple, with all of them reporting afterward on how they found the experience. Presented by Gyles Brandreth(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The team tries to find relatives of a travelling salesman who lived in Scotland. Meanwhile, the search for heirs of a retired dressmaker uncovers tales of boxing and bigamy, and the tragic story of a family decimated by the holocaust. Plus, details of two unclaimed estates where heirs still need to be found(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits archaeological digs around the UK, starting with the west of Britain. Finds include the camp from which Vikings invaded Britain and groundbreaking new evidence that the world famous Avebury stone circle isn't just a sacred site, but a place where our ancestors lived and worked(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actress Sheridan Smith, who went from singing with her parents in the working men's clubs near their North Lincolnshire home to successful stage performances in Little Shop of Horrors and Legally Blonde - not to mention her countless TV appearances - embarks on a journey to find out where her family's musical talents come from. She is soon hot on the trail of her great-great-grandfather Benjamin Doubleday, a world-class banjo player and musical impresario - but she is surprised by his change in fortunes(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Norwich Cathedral's great nave, experts Kate Bateman and David Fletcher pick out some choice items to take to auction. Among them are an enamel-topped silver trinket box and a vintage model car. Plus, Paul Martin uncovers the meaning of religious graffiti(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Sam & Shauna's Big Cook Out
programme.desc: Samantha Evans and Shauna Guinn travel to the small town of Deri near Caerphilly to celebrate the success of its rugby club, which has gone from the brink of extinction just four years ago to being crowned league champions. To mark the achievements of the players and the volunteers who run the club, Sam and Shauna cook up a huge venison feast with their help(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal worker Dominic Chinea has to use all of his restoration skills to future proof an old, Cornish museum sign, while Steven Fletcher has to steer an old banger back onto the road. Plus, bear-care team Julie and Amanda start treatment on George, a very special teddy who has lost one of his eyes and his growl(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys grills four more contenders on their chosen subjects, which are the history of the London Underground, the 1969 Woodstock festival, the industrialist William Armstrong and the life and films of Al Pacino. They are then each afforded two minutes to demonstrate their prowess on general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don assesses the progress, successes and failures of the paradise garden he planted at the start of the year. He also sets about sowing a meadow of wildflowers in the orchard and gives advice on what to plant now in the vegetable garden. Frances Tophill travels to Glasgow to meet a gardener whose hands-off approach to his garden has reaped rewards for wildlife, Adam Frost visits a garden in Wales where a potentially unpromising and rocky site has been transformed, while Mark Lane explores Nymans in West Sussex to find out how the garden is managed with sustainability at its heart(n)

programme.title: Inside the Vatican
programme.desc: A film crew gains exceptional access to Vatican, afforded a year to examine its inner workings. Cameras capture everyone including Pope Francis himself, his head of security, the nuns who cater for the homeless, the choristers of the Sistine Chapel, the papal gardener and the chief of the diplomatic corps to gain a unique and exclusive insight into the lives of those who live and work in this independent City State. The first of two programmes follows the build-up to Easter, when tens of thousands of pilgrims arrive and Pope Francis takes centre stage in the world's biggest theatre of faith(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig 'quarrels', with regular panellist Alan Davies and guest players Jason Manford, Aisling Bea and Anuvab Pal at the brunt of her angst. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's national and international news stories, with Katie Razzall(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Mary Beard talks to novelist Margaret Atwood as her sequel to The Handmaid's Tale - The Testaments - is published, Their far-reaching conversation touches upon how Gilead has changed almost 35 years on from the original book and how the cloak featured in the TV adaptation of The Handmaid's Tale has become a symbol of protest around the world. Plus, Atwood shares her responses to the current political climate, about the accusation that she is a 'bad feminist'; and the hype surrounding the release of this new book(n)

programme.title: The Lunchbox (2013)
programme.desc: A neglected wife hopes to get some appreciation from her husband by making him packed lunches containing love letters and sending them to his workplace. However, the packages are accidentally given to the wrong employee, who responds to the messages and becomes a close and trusted confidante. Romantic drama, starring Irrfan Khan and Nimrat Kaur. In Hindi and English(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: The Brecon Beacons with Iolo Williams
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores the unique scenery of the Brecon Beacons over the course of a year, braving the elements in every season to detail the ever-changing landscape of the valleys. He begins in the winter, enduring a raging blizzard on the high peaks while the lowlands are bathed in sun. On the snowy slopes, foxes search for food while the great grey shrike - a ruthless hunter from Scandinavia - seeks other animals to eat. Plus, Iolo tracks red deer to the secluded gullies in one of the Beacons' wildest locations(n)

programme.title: Dig WW2 with Dan Snow
programme.desc: The historian explores Northern Ireland's role in the Second World War, revealing the story of a pilot who became the most successful U-boat hunter of all time. The programme also examines Harland & Wolff's contributions to the development of the famous Churchill tank, and takes an underwater look at the wreck of the U-155. In Donegal, a major excavation reveals the 70-year-old Spitfire flown by American pilot Bud Wolfe, who was forced to bail out just minutes after taking off(n)

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: Dr Janina Ramirez heads to Mexico, where, just before the outbreak of the Second World War, American husband-and-wife explorer team Matthew and Marion Stirling were lured into the jungle by the legend of a colossal stone head. They found the head - and a lot more than they bargained for - because it turned out to be the first clue in a trail that led to the discovery of a lost civilisation, now known as the Olmecs(n)

programme.title: Vikings
programme.desc: Neil Oliver continues his attempt to shed new light on the Norse warriors by examining their sophisticated trading skills, and visits locations in Russia, Turkey and Ireland to explain how they established a business empire. He also explores the vast array of goods that were bought and sold by Viking merchants and settlers, including Chinese silks and Pictish slaves(n)

programme.title: Stories from the Dark Earth: Meet the Ancestors Revisited
programme.desc: Archaeologist Julian Richards revisits some of his most important digs to discover how science, conservation and new finds have changed people's understanding of ancient history. His journey begins at the excavation of two burials from Roman Britain - a wealthy man from Winchester, Hampshire, and the lavishly appointed grave of a woman from the heart of London(n)

programme.title: Sicily: Wonder of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Michael Scott concludes his history of Sicily, telling the story of how the Christian Byzantine Empire was swept aside by a Muslim Arab invasion from North Africa. The island was transformed by new trade networks and agricultural techniques, and in the 19th century was unified with Italy - but the greatest threat to their prosperity then came from within and the rise of the Mafia(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The war with the Lees escalates when they ransack the Shelbys' betting shop, while John reveals he intends to remarry, but his choice of bride does not go down well with his family. Chief Inspector Campbell puts more pressure on Thomas to deliver Freddie Thorne and the crate of stolen guns to him, while Grace begins to glean more information about the Peaky Blinders' illegal operations(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Thomas deals with an IRA chief who has come to Small Heath to avenge his cousin's death. Meanwhile, Campbell gets closer to finding the stolen guns and Grace has to decide whether her loyalties lie with the inspector or with Thomas. Period gangster drama, starring Cillian Murphy and Sam Neill(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Thomas prepares to oust Billy Kimber at Worcester Racecourse, but secrets are revealed that force the Shelbys to face up to the problems that have divided them. Meanwhile, Campbell unleashes one last plan to destroy the Peaky Blinders and Grace finds herself with an impossible decision to make. Drama, starring Cillian Murphy and Sam Neill(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Peter Powell and Simon Mayo present the edition that first aired on June 9, featuring the Timelords, Five Star, Voice of the Beehive, Mica Paris, Fields of the Nephilim, Sabrina, James Brown, Maxi Priest, Morrissey, Wet Wet Wet and Erasure(n)

programme.title: Maps: Power, Plunder and Possession
programme.desc: Professor Jerry Brotton explains how maps encouraged the plunder and conquest of far-off lands as fabled riches drove explorers such as Christopher Columbus and Walter Raleigh to the New World. He examines the founding of America and the myth of Eldorado, before discussing how cartography made international enterprise possible through the Dutch East India Company and similar ventures(n)

programme.title: Hyundai Mercury Prize 2019 Live: Album of the Year
programme.desc: Lauren Laverne hosts the annual ceremony to announce the best album of the year, live from the Eventim Apollo, Hammersmith. The 12 shortlisted acts are Anna Calvi, Black Midi, Cate Le Bon, Dave, Foals, Fontaines DC, Idles, Little Simz, Nao, SEED Ensemble, Slowthai and the 1975(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Planets are now being discovered outside Earth's solar system on a regular basis, and these strange new worlds are forcing scientists to rewrite the history of how the Sun and the planets, satellites, dwarf planets, asteroids and comets that orbit it came into being. This programme reveals that far from a simple story of stable objects orbiting a star, the development of the solar system was a potent combination of hellfire, chaos and planetary pinball that makes it somewhat of a miracle that Earth was created at all(n)

programme.title: Land of the Lost Wolves
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan presents a two-part programme documenting the return of wolves to the snowy peaks of Washington's Cascade Mountains. He charts the fortunes of first pack to live in the north-west of America in 70 years after all but being wiped out across the country(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988

programme.title: Peaky Blinders

programme.title: A Musical History
programme.desc: Fleetwood Mac are the great survivors of British and American rock music. For more than 50 years they've overcome break-ups and breakdowns to become one of the most successful bands of all time, selling over 100 million records worldwide, with their 1977 smash Rumours accounting for nearly half of those sales. Here, fan Edith Bowman provides a narrative overview, alongside fellow celebrity admirers who all pay tribute to the band. Contributors include KT Tunstall, Travis' frontman Fran Healy, Toyah Willcox, Sian Pattenden and Emma Dabiri(n)

programme.title: Fleetwood Mac's Songbird: Christine McVie
programme.desc: While she has never fronted Fleetwood Mac, preferring to align herself with 'the boys' in the rhythm section, Christine McVie is their most successful singer-songwriter whose hits include Over My Head, Don't Stop and Everywhere. After global success in the late '70s and mid-'80s Christine left the band in 1998, living in semi-retirement in Kent, only to rejoin in 2014. Here, she finally gets to take centre stage to tell both her own story and that of Fleetwood Mac from her vantage point. Contributors include Christine's current bandmates Stevie Nicks, Mick Fleetwood, John McVie, Neil Finn and Mike Campbell(n)

programme.title: Fleetwood Mac - Don't Stop
programme.desc: Documentary looking back over the band's long career, from the early blues outfit led by Peter Green in the late 1960s, to their reincarnation in the 1970s with the line-up that went on to record the 40-million-selling album Rumours. Mick Fleetwood, John McVie, Lindsey Buckingham and Stevie Nicks reveal the truth behind the successes, failures and relationships that characterised their music and made them one of the most enduring acts in popular music(n)

programme.desc: Jack and Salty are lost in the Antarctic, and only one friend can save them - the little penguin(n)

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: The school's teacher is retiring, which worries Katie, on top of news that her mum and dad are having another baby(n)

programme.title: Melody
programme.desc: Mum and Melody fly a kite, and later the youngster asks for some music about hills or mountains(n)

programme.title: Biggleton
programme.desc: Freezegirl helps the people of Biggleton to rescue a stolen statue. Pre-school live action series, narrated by Eamonn Holmes(n)

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Letter A discovers a magic red nose and gets up to all sorts of five-letter mischief, introducing the words prank, stink, trunk, stomp, stamp, plank and stunt(n)

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say 'bed' and goes on a whirlwind word adventure, featuring comfy, jumpy, big, baby and cosy beds(n)

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: One of the bottles in Ten's collection accidentally falls off the wall, setting off a spectacular numbery hullabaloo(n)

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Flop and Bing find a patch of fluffy white dandelions in the park, and Bing decides to take one as a present for Sula(n)

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: Things get out of hand when Chickedy and Chick try to turn the pedals of a bike. The Twirlywoo Screen provides the Twirlywoos with a cycle of their own, but it proves difficult to control(n)

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends visit a hat museum, while Mr Tumble tries out some tricks with a magical one(n)

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies eat Tubby Custard and ride the Custard Train around Home Dome, while a child in Tummy Tales tries to make his baby sister laugh(n)

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear meet babies Isobel, Idris, Lincoln, Amber, Amelia, Jason and Willow.(n)

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: When Mum leaves a can of air freshener on the counter, it's up to Pablo and his friends to stop The Smell Gobbler eating all the smells in the house - including the nice ones(n)

programme.title: My First
programme.desc: Cameras follow Joseph as he goes on his first camping trip to the Lake District with his family(n)

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day travels back three million years where he tracks down the ancient tribe of australopithecus primates, but he has his trusty backpack stolen by hungry vultures(n)

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Join Gem's daring Swashbucklers as they attempt to win back her jewels from the naughty pirates Cook, Line and Captain Sinker, in this hilarious and fast-paced physical gameshow. Today's Swashbucklers; Aisha, Holly, Kunda and Mariyam take on the Cocounutty Raft and Bouncy Sails in pursuit of Gem's jewels. Captain Sinker has found a precious pearl in a stinky oyster so Cook and Line think there must be precious things in anything that's smelly!(n)

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Whoooooo the shamanistic owl looks down, and the squirrels look for ways to cheer her up(n)

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Messy has a pair of green socks waiting for him. He eats one, but loses the second and sets off to find it in Okido(n)

programme.title: Catie's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: CBeebies rally driver Catie shows off three amazing machines that are all really tall(n)

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: The predators come up with a plan to get rid of Peter Rabbit because they are tired of being constantly humiliated(n)

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world(n)

programme.title: Gigglebiz
programme.desc: DIY Dan attempts to make a potted plant display, while Rapids Johnson tries out a new technique to try to find bears in the woods(n)

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Animated fun for younger viewers featuring tales of the world's worst puppy who woofs and wags his way into the lives of an unassuming family(n)

programme.title: Time for School
programme.desc: Observational documentary on the lives of reception children at two contrasting primary schools - one rural, and one urban(n)

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Michael Palin narrates a revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. The Music Tree notes for Tiny Clanger's marvellous new melody are blown away by a strong wind and no-one can find them(n)

programme.title: Gareth Thomas: HIV and Me
programme.desc: Documentary following former Wales rugby star Gareth Thomas as he opens up about living with HIV and sets about tackling the stigmas, myths and misunderstandings surrounding the virus. The programme follows Gareth as he prepares to take on the biggest physical challenge of his career - running the world's toughest Iron Man(n)

programme.title: Japan with Sue Perkins
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sue Perkins embarks upon a trip around Japan, exploring a nation caught between the demands of a hi-tech future and the pull of a traditional past. Sue begins her journey in the capital city of Tokyo, where she trains with a female sumo wrestling team, before visiting a family who live with robots. Then, she leaves the city behind to see the a different side of Japan, and explore its forests, mountains and islands(n)

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace examines the ways supermarkets fight for customers' loyalty, and visits a farm claiming to be using technology to produce healthy chicken at a low cost. Elsewhere, Gregg meets Asda's head of fruit and vegetables to find out why it is a challenge to remove plastic packaging from products, and Nikki Fox sees how they are using a virtual supermarket to see what appeals to us most(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple who love the outdoors find the perfect rural family home within reach of the coast, on a budget of £850,000(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich Brothers and Charlie Dimmock compete to create a garden in Chester that combines exotic holiday memories of Borneo, America and South Africa for owners Michelle and Andrew(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton's penultimate leg takes them to Lowestoft and Cromer. Along the way, Izzie is charmed by a snake and an art deco necklace, while James goes crabbing and gets all fired up by a cannonball. But who will come out on top at auction in Banbury?(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bobby tries to come to terms with everything that has happened - only to find himself in a dangerous situation. Jay lets Mel into the funeral parlour to be with Hunter - and when Lisa finds her, the two take a trip down memory lane. Bex tries to open up to a drunken Robbie, and Keegan arranges a romantic meal for Tiffany(n)

programme.title: Watchdog
programme.desc: Matt Allwright, Steph McGovern and Nikki Fox present consumer investigations, including research into which airlines rank best and worst for cost, complaints and customer service. Three families put leading fitness trackers to some rigorous real world testing, there's a look at how high street names are targeting consumers with unsolicited adverts, and in Rogue Traders, a roofer is caught charging thousands of pounds for poor work(n)

programme.title: The Cameron Years
programme.desc: The first in a two-part documentary drawing on an interview with former prime minister David Cameron to explore his leadership, the events that led to the EU referendum and its impact on the political landscape - not to mention his own career. The first film examines how the prime minister went from riding high after the successful 2015 election campaign to defeat in the referendum. Including contributions from Nick Clegg, Iain Duncan Smith, Michael Gove, George Osborne, Ann Widdecombe and Michael Heseltine(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Southampton, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: The second edition of the TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU. And after a dramatic few weeks in the Commons as the deadline approaches, they'll no doubt have much to talk about(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley visits the Metropolitan Police's Mounted Unit, who are celebrating 100 years at their Surrey training centre. Back in the studio, Rav Wilding has the latest appeals for viewers' help(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An update on an unusual project in Capel Le Ferne in Kent that Lucy Alexander first visited in 2009. Plus, a mid-terrace house in Liverpool and a cottage in Plaistow, east London(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The enforcement officers investigate the tragic death of a Red Arrows pilot, and they smash a puppy-smuggling operation. A supermarket is fined £8million after a massive fuel leak at one of its petrol stations has a devastating impact on the local community and environment.(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles tells the story of a paraglider hanging by a thread in a forest after his canopy collapses. A dash cam captures the moment three cars collide on a mountain road, and a bullock on the pitch nearly hits a village cricket team for six(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: As Emma's dreams escalate, Zara has a radical idea about how to stop them, and Becky puts Daniel on the spot. Ayesha deals with the fallout when an angry patient ignores the warning signs that his health is in danger(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Seventeen contenders start this edition of the quiz, but how many of them will survive to face the £10,000 Question? Hosted by Rick Edwards(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton accompanies a couple on a North Yorkshire house hunt. They are calling time on running a guest house in the city and have a £650 000 budget to find a rural family home with business potential. Whilst in the county, Alistair also pays a visit to a heritage train line and helps the team to get their impressive locomotives fired up for the start of the tourist season(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Oxfordshire mum Sarah is after a space where she can relax and unwind after a full-on day minding two young children, but is also aware that the garden also needs to work as a play space for the youngsters. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to Bicester to assist Sarah and partner Adam, who need a garden the whole family can play and relax in(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton tour Izzie's native Derbyshire on the final leg, where she tempts the gourmand with some local delicacies. There's a blast from the past as James is carried away by the news from the 1780s while Izzie follows in the tracks of Winston Churchill. Then, it's onwards to auction in Lincoln(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Iolo's Street Life
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores the wild side of towns and finds birds and animals that have adapted to life on the street. In the opening programme, he stumbles on tawny owls in residence in an Abertillery chimney a thriving colony of rats in the centre of Swansea and a fox family living in an allotment in Cardiff. Iolo also finds the best fed swans in Wales, while a heron visits a Llanberis garden for his daily diet of junk food(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Burnley defender James Tarkowski, Great Britain sprinter Asha Philip, IPC World Championships long jump champion Stef Reid and Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Michael Jamieson join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Lisa supports Mel as she prepares for Hunter's funeral. Over at the Mitchells', however, Phil is adamant that none of his family will attend, leaving Louise conflicted - and things only get worse when Phil realises that Jay went behind his back to help Mel. Meanwhile, Tiffany and Keegan agree to take things slowly, while Patrick is determined to talk to Ted about making a pass at Sheree - only to realise he misses Joyce(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The latest battle for a semi-final place reaches its climax as the four celebrities are tasked to cook a faultless two-course meal that will not only be judged by John Torode and Gregg Wallace but also by former contenders Lisa Faulkner, Kimberly Wyatt and Adrian Edmondson. Only the best two contestants can come one step closer to winning the title of Celebrity MasterChef 2019. For those who don't make the grade, the journey ends here(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: On the day of Mrs Bookham's funeral, a blazing row about the contents of the deceased's handbag sees Lisa Marie storm out telling Geraldine she can 'stick her job'. At the arcade Mike receives a visit from a disgruntled Tony Peroni who wants to call in his debt, while suspicions mounts about Bigsy's involvement in Mr Ferris's burglary. Karen has doubts about her relationship with Mike and during a heart to heart with Lisa Marie confesses that there was a lot more to the kiss between Mike and Hayley Cox. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole, Maggie Ollerenshaw, Catherine Tyldesley and Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen references clips from half a century and more of Welsh television as he sets out on a quest to discover the truth about rugby. It's a story of vodka, Groggs and adventures away, full of lively characters from the nation's sporting past.(n)

programme.title: Room 101 - Extra Storage
programme.desc: Extended edition of the comedy panel show presented by Frank Skinner, with Richard Ayoade, Alun Cochrane and Gabby Logan competing to have their pet hates consigned to Room 101, with topics including people who laugh out loud when reading a book, fun and half and half scarves(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: An elderly amateur sleuth who was a friend of Danny's is murdered, and he ends up embroiled in the case when his photo is found at the scene. However, the cold case copper becomes convinced that the secret to uncovering her real killer lies within the last case she was working on. Picking up where she left off, he travels to the village of Minchampton, where a woman was said to have been murdered by a local lad 25 years previously. Meanwhile, Sasha is left to cope with the investigating officer on the modern-day Miss Marple's murder - an overbearing contemporary from her Hendon training, by the name of DCI Grace Mackie - and Steve lets Gerry stay with him when he is evicted, but soon comes to regret the act of kindness(n)

And after a dramatic few weeks in the Commons as the deadline approaches, they'll no doubt have much to talk about(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley visits the Metropolitan Police's Mounted Unit, who are celebrating 100 years at their Surrey training centre. Back in the studio, Rav Wilding has the latest appeals for viewers' help(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An update on an unusual project in Capel Le Ferne in Kent that Lucy Alexander first visited in 2009. Plus, a mid-terrace house in Liverpool and a cottage in Plaistow, east London(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The enforcement officers investigate the tragic death of a Red Arrows pilot, and they smash a puppy-smuggling operation. A supermarket is fined £8million after a massive fuel leak at one of its petrol stations has a devastating impact on the local community and environment.(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles tells the story of a paraglider hanging by a thread in a forest after his canopy collapses. A dash cam captures the moment three cars collide on a mountain road, and a bullock on the pitch nearly hits a village cricket team for six(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: As Emma's dreams escalate, Zara has a radical idea about how to stop them, and Becky puts Daniel on the spot. Ayesha deals with the fallout when an angry patient ignores the warning signs that his health is in danger(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Seventeen contenders start this edition of the quiz, but how many of them will survive to face the £10,000 Question? Hosted by Rick Edwards(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton accompanies a couple on a North Yorkshire house hunt. They are calling time on running a guest house in the city and have a £650 000 budget to find a rural family home with business potential. Whilst in the county, Alistair also pays a visit to a heritage train line and helps the team to get their impressive locomotives fired up for the start of the tourist season(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Oxfordshire mum Sarah is after a space where she can relax and unwind after a full-on day minding two young children, but is also aware that the garden also needs to work as a play space for the youngsters. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to Bicester to assist Sarah and partner Adam, who need a garden the whole family can play and relax in(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton tour Izzie's native Derbyshire on the final leg, where she tempts the gourmand with some local delicacies. There's a blast from the past as James is carried away by the news from the 1780s while Izzie follows in the tracks of Winston Churchill. Then, it's onwards to auction in Lincoln(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: The team returns with stories from the heart of the Scottish countryside. Dougie Vipond asks if Scottish livestock farming can survive climate change, while Anne Lundon visits the farmer's fields being transformed into a final resting place. Euan McIlwraith sails out from Mull in search of the secretive basking shark and Arlene Stuart discovers the fruitful history of apple growing on Clydeside. Plus, Nick Nairn parks the Landward food van in Moffat, where his ingredients include air-dried jerky, Scottish-grown chillis and prime Galloway beef(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Burnley defender James Tarkowski, Great Britain sprinter Asha Philip, IPC World Championships long jump champion Stef Reid and Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Michael Jamieson join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Lisa supports Mel as she prepares for Hunter's funeral. Over at the Mitchells', however, Phil is adamant that none of his family will attend, leaving Louise conflicted - and things only get worse when Phil realises that Jay went behind his back to help Mel. Meanwhile, Tiffany and Keegan agree to take things slowly, while Patrick is determined to talk to Ted about making a pass at Sheree - only to realise he misses Joyce(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The latest battle for a semi-final place reaches its climax as the four celebrities are tasked to cook a faultless two-course meal that will not only be judged by John Torode and Gregg Wallace but also by former contenders Lisa Faulkner, Kimberly Wyatt and Adrian Edmondson. Only the best two contestants can come one step closer to winning the title of Celebrity MasterChef 2019. For those who don't make the grade, the journey ends here(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: On the day of Mrs Bookham's funeral, a blazing row about the contents of the deceased's handbag sees Lisa Marie storm out telling Geraldine she can 'stick her job'. At the arcade Mike receives a visit from a disgruntled Tony Peroni who wants to call in his debt, while suspicions mounts about Bigsy's involvement in Mr Ferris's burglary. Karen has doubts about her relationship with Mike and during a heart to heart with Lisa Marie confesses that there was a lot more to the kiss between Mike and Hayley Cox. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole, Maggie Ollerenshaw, Catherine Tyldesley and Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Room 101 - Extra Storage
programme.desc: Extended edition of the comedy panel show presented by Frank Skinner, with Richard Ayoade, Alun Cochrane and Gabby Logan competing to have their pet hates consigned to Room 101, with topics including people who laugh out loud when reading a book, fun and half and half scarves(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: An elderly amateur sleuth who was a friend of Danny's is murdered, and he ends up embroiled in the case when his photo is found at the scene. However, the cold case copper becomes convinced that the secret to uncovering her real killer lies within the last case she was working on. Picking up where she left off, he travels to the village of Minchampton, where a woman was said to have been murdered by a local lad 25 years previously. Meanwhile, Sasha is left to cope with the investigating officer on the modern-day Miss Marple's murder - an overbearing contemporary from her Hendon training, by the name of DCI Grace Mackie - and Steve lets Gerry stay with him when he is evicted, but soon comes to regret the act of kindness(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts revisits an old church in Denby, Derbyshire, which was being turned into a home in 2007, and there are also visits to Milton Regis in Kent and Liverpool(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers investigate a factory fire that forces residents to evacuate their homes, and a tipper truck is caught on camera as it illegally dumps 20 tons of waste(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A professional British cyclist is involved in a horrific collision with a race support vehicle, and two men using an accelerant to start a bonfire get more than they bargained for(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ruhma and Jimmi are there for baby Josh when he needs them most, while Al gets more than he bargained for when Eve confronts him about his underhand tactics(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple looking for a home in rural Cambridgeshire, and also visits National Trust nature reserve Wicken Fen - one of the most important wetlands in Europe.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden in Northampton that will allow two children with limited mobility access to the outdoor space(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton head to Lincolnshire, Cambridgeshire and Bedfordshire. James tries out a local delicacy and Izzie tries her hand at a winter sport(n)

programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The four remaining contestants face two more culinary challenges, as they compete to secure a place in the semi-finals. First, the celebrities are split into two teams of two, and each team is tasked with cooking the same dish, which must be identical in both appearance and flavour. For the second challenge, the competitors are then sent out on their first mass catering challenge, taking place at the Elizabeth Garrett Anderson school in North London(n)

programme.title: Japan with Sue Perkins
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sue Perkins embarks upon a trip around Japan, exploring a nation caught between the demands of a hi-tech future and the pull of a traditional past. Sue begins her journey in the capital city of Tokyo, where she trains with a female sumo wrestling team, before visiting a family who live with robots. Then, she leaves the city behind to see the a different side of Japan, and explore its forests, mountains and islands(n)

programme.title: Nolan Live
programme.desc: Stephen Nolan probes the issues that matter to the people of Northern Ireland, focusing on the stories of the week and the people making the headlines in lively debates with the studio audience and guest panellists(n)

programme.title: Gareth Thomas: HIV and Me
programme.desc: Documentary following former Wales rugby star Gareth Thomas as he opens up about living with HIV and sets about tackling the stigmas, myths and misunderstandings surrounding the virus. The programme follows Gareth as he prepares to take on the biggest physical challenge of his career - running the world's toughest Iron Man(n)

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace examines the ways supermarkets fight for customers' loyalty, and visits a farm claiming to be using technology to produce healthy chicken at a low cost. Elsewhere, Gregg meets Asda's head of fruit and vegetables to find out why it is a challenge to remove plastic packaging from products, and Nikki Fox sees how they are using a virtual supermarket to see what appeals to us most(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley finds out how crime affects the farming community of Warwickshire and sees some of the technology that helps police track down stolen livestock(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development progress of terraced houses in Carlise and Abbey Wood in London, as well as a seven-year project to renovate a large period house in Torquay(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers investigate the death of a car wash worker, and catch two crooks dealing in counterfeit medicines worth £5million(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A driver whose dashcam recorded his heart attack and rescue by other motorists, and the rescuers who rushed to the aid of people swept out to sea, only to be dragged in themselves(n)

programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Tim Weeks provide expert advice to two teams as the competition heads to the Royal Cornwall Showground in Wadebridge. Plus, presenter Anita Manning shows of a Bargain Hunt favourite with a Cornish flavour(n)

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al seeks Zara's advice on making amends with his mother and Becky unearths the tragic secret behind an unusual occurrence at her grandma sheltered housing complex. Emma tries to prevent Karen finding out she has been dreaming about Rob(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple who love the outdoors find the perfect rural family home within reach of the coast, on a budget of £850,000(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich Brothers and Charlie Dimmock compete to create a garden in Chester that combines exotic holiday memories of Borneo, America and South Africa for owners Michelle and Andrew(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton's penultimate leg takes them to Lowestoft and Cromer. Along the way, Izzie is charmed by a snake and an art deco necklace, while James goes crabbing and gets all fired up by a cannonball. But who will come out on top at auction in Banbury?(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bobby tries to come to terms with everything that has happened - only to find himself in a dangerous situation. Jay lets Mel into the funeral parlour to be with Hunter - and when Lisa finds her, the two take a trip down memory lane. Bex tries to open up to a drunken Robbie, and Keegan arranges a romantic meal for Tiffany(n)

programme.title: Watchdog
programme.desc: Matt Allwright, Steph McGovern and Nikki Fox present consumer investigations, including research into which airlines rank best and worst for cost, complaints and customer service. Three families put leading fitness trackers to some rigorous real world testing, there's a look at how high street names are targeting consumers with unsolicited adverts, and in Rogue Traders, a roofer is caught charging thousands of pounds for poor work(n)

programme.title: The Cameron Years
programme.desc: The first in a two-part documentary drawing on an interview with former prime minister David Cameron to explore his leadership, the events that led to the EU referendum and its impact on the political landscape - not to mention his own career. The first film examines how the prime minister went from riding high after the successful 2015 election campaign to defeat in the referendum. Including contributions from Nick Clegg, Iain Duncan Smith, Michael Gove, George Osborne, Ann Widdecombe and Michael Heseltine(n)

programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich brothers and Arit Anderson travel to Shefford in Bedfordshire, where they meet a couple who claim their garden is basic and plain. Duncan would like it to be modern, but also contain elements of a rustic, cottage garden. Both he and Karen like the idea of a curved patio and wish to avoid decking. Will the couple choose the Rich brothers proposed design, complete with bespoke veg beds, pair of curved hedges and paved courtyard, or will Arit's design that offers a new circular lawn, choice of paved seating areas and a vertical waterfall be more to their taste?(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Live
programme.desc: Japan v Russia (Kick-off 11.45am). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the opening match of the tournament, as the Pool A sides meet at Tokyo Stadium. Although they would ultimately miss out on progressing to the group stage, the hosts were one of the surprise packages in the last staging of the competition four years ago, producing one of the biggest shocks in World Cup history when they overcame South Africa 34-32 in their opener. They will be looking for another tournament to remember on home soil and start as favourites against the Russians, whose only previous appearance in a finals was in 2011, when they lost all four pool fixtures. programme.desc: Billy's final leg begins in Virginia on the trail of bootleggers dealing in moonshine before he hears the story of the amazing woman who led hundreds of slaves to their freedom. programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Live
programme.desc: Japan v Russia (Kick-off 11.45am). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the opening match of the tournament, as the Pool A sides meet at Tokyo Stadium. Although they would ultimately miss out on progressing to the group stage, the hosts were one of the surprise packages in the last staging of the competition four years ago, producing one of the biggest shocks in World Cup history when they overcame South Africa 34-32 in their opener. They will be looking for another tournament to remember on home soil and start as favourites against the Russians, whose only previous appearance in a finals was in 2011, when they lost all four pool fixtures. With analysis from Jonny Wilkinson, Brian O'Driscoll and Sam Warburton, reports by Jill Douglas and Bryan Habana and commentary from Miles Harrison, Shane Williams and Jamie Roberts(n)
